task_desc: Add educational guide panel with section-by-section brain region explanations -- Create a toggleable educational panel (slide-out sidebar or modal) that explains the fly brain to non-scientists. The panel should contain: (1) An introduction explaining that this is a simplified ~70-group functional model of the Drosophila melanogaster brain (real brain has ~130K neurons and ~50M connections), why it's grouped this way, and what "connectome" means, (2) A section for each major brain region with: a plain-language explanation of what it does, a real-world analogy (e.g., "The mushroom bodies are like the fly's hippocampus -- they form and recall memories about smells"), which user interactions activate it (e.g., "Feed the fly and watch the SEZ light up"), the list of neuron groups in our model that belong to this region with population estimates from FlyWire data, (3) A "Signal Flow" section explaining sensory-input -> central-processing -> motor-output with an interactive diagram (can be SVG or canvas-drawn) showing the major pathways, (4) A "What's Missing" section honestly explaining what the 70-group model simplifies: no individual neuron dynamics, no synaptic plasticity, no neuromodulator diffusion, no electrical synapses (gap junctions), and approximate rather than exact connection weights, (5) A "Learn More" section with links to FlyWire (codex.flywire.ai), the Dorkenwald et al. 2024 paper, Virtual Fly Brain (virtualflybrain.org), and the original worm-sim that inspired this project. Add a "?" or "Learn" button to the toolbar that opens this panel. Clicking a region name in the guide should highlight the corresponding region in the 3D brain view (if open) by temporarily boosting its emissive glow. Files: index.html, js/education.js (new), css/main.css.
